title: China's Coal Production Remains Strongest
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
China's coal production averaged 367.1 million tons in January/February. This marks a decline of 35.6 million tons (-9%) from December's record production but is up year-on-year by 23.8 million tons (7%). As we have been stressing in our work, the government last year shifted its focus to ensuring robust coal production rather than focusing on mine safety.
